raw_consumer_statement:
I received a voicemail from LL Bean Citibank Mastercard on XX/XX/XXXX, saying I owed them a payment for charges on the card. I never received a card from them. In late XXXX of XXXX, I signed up for an LL Bean Mastercard at the store in XXXXXXXX XXXX in order to get a store discount. 

In early XXXX, on or close to XX/XX/XXXX, I paid off the charges using the online account for the in store purchase, but still did not receive a card. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I called LL Bean Citibank Mastercard customer service to ask about a payment they referenced on the voicemail. They started reading off all of these daily charges over {$400.00} per day, to a total of roughly XXXX XXXXXXXX. I told them these were not my charges and it must be fraud. I was then transferred to the fraud department. The fraud department said these did look fraudulent, since they were significant, daily and not in a state I live, they were in XXXX. I was told they would investigate and to ignore any asks for payment. 

Since then, I have been bombarded with automatic voicemails asking for payment. Concerned, I called on the week of XX/XX/XXXX to ask them to stop and freeze everything until the investigation was complete. Then I was transferred to the fraud department, who confirmed they would send me an email regarding the case. They never did this. 

I received a recent bill in the mail as well, listing all the charges. On XX/XX/XXXX, I decided to file a police report, showing them the charges. The week of XX/XX/XXXX, I have now alerted all of the credit bureau 's since my credit score has now dropped significantly. Prior, I had XXXX XXXXXXXX 

I have now received a letter from LL Bean Citibank Mastercard saying they reviewed the charges and decided I should be responsible for payment. It is clearly fraud, and all of these charges are not from my state or any place I ever buy products from.
